to prevent < and > inside LaTeX $...$ from being parsed as HTML tags.

- renderQuizQuestion: q.q, opts, explain
- showQuizResult: wrong answer review
- renderKQQuestion: already done (verified)
- KQ result review: already done (verified)
- renderQBank: q.q, opts, blanks, explain
- renderExamPaper: q.q, opts, blanks, explain
- renderGradedQuestion: q.q, opts, blanks, explain
- Score detail modal: q.q, explain

* Fix Wolverine shutdown exception filter to handle nested AggregateException

The existing catch clause checked only direct InnerExceptions, so it missed
cases where Wolverine wrapped the ObjectDisposedException(EventLogInternal)
inside another AggregateException during DrainAsync.

The shutdown sequence is:
  1. AMQP link drain exceeds 3-second budget  TimeoutException (logged by ListeningAgent)
  2. Wolverine calls LogError() to record the timeout  EventLogInternal is
     already disposed  ThrowLoggingError() raises AggregateException(ObjectDisposedException)
  3. Wolverine's DrainAsync wraps that in a second AggregateException before
     re-throwing, so the filter never matched.

Fix: call Flatten() before the All() check, and add TimeoutException to the
allowed set so both leaf exception types from the inline-listener teardown
path are suppressed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix handler test to verify PublishAsync instead of ScheduleAsync extension method

ScheduleAsync<T>(msg, TimeSpan) is a static extension method in Wolverine's
MessageBusExtensions  Moq cannot set up or verify extension methods because
they are not virtual interface members.

The extension method delegates directly to IMessageBus.PublishAsync<T>(msg, options)
with options.ScheduleDelay set to the requested delay, so the correct thing
to verify is PublishAsync with a DeliveryOptions matcher.

commit.message:
feat(mcp): filter SafeOutputs tools based on front matter config (#156)

* feat(mcp): filter SafeOutputs tools based on front matter config

Only expose safe output tools that are configured in the front matter's
safe-outputs section. This reduces agent confusion by hiding tools that
aren't configured for the current pipeline.

Implementation:
- Add --enabled-tools repeatable CLI arg to mcp and mcp-http commands
- SafeOutputs::new() accepts optional enabled tools list and uses
  ToolRouter::remove_route() to remove unconfigured tools at startup
- Compiler derives tool list from safe-outputs keys and emits
  --enabled-tools args in the pipeline template
- Always-on diagnostic tools (noop, missing-data, missing-tool,
  report-incomplete) are never filtered out

Backward compatible: if --enabled-tools is not passed (empty
safe-outputs or omitted), all tools remain available.

Note: MCPG has a tools field in its config schema but does not enforce
it at runtime. This change filters at the SafeOutputs server level
instead, making it self-contained.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for SafeOutputs tool filtering

- Validate tool names against safe regex (ASCII alphanumeric + hyphens)
  to prevent shell injection from malicious YAML keys
- Fix dangling backslash in base.yml when enabled_tools_args is empty
- Replace fragile exact-count assertion in test_tool_filtering_multiple_tools
  with explicit presence/absence checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: trailing space for enabled_tools_args and move ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS

- Add trailing space to generate_enabled_tools_args output when non-empty,
  preventing the last --enabled-tools value from concatenating with the
  next positional argument in the shell command
- Move ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS constant from mcp.rs to tools/mod.rs to avoid
  compile→mcp coupling (common.rs now imports from tools directly)
- Reduce list_all() calls in tool filtering to a single collect pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: warn on unrecognized safe-output keys at compile time

- Add ALL_KNOWN_SAFE_OUTPUTS constant in tools/mod.rs enumerating every
  valid safe-output key (MCP tools + always-on diagnostics + memory)
- Emit compile-time warning when a safe-outputs key doesn't match any
  known tool, catching typos like 'crate-pull-request' early
- Use HashSet for O(1) deduplication when merging always-on tools
- Rename misleading test to test_generate_enabled_tools_args_warns_on_unknown_tool
  and exercise the typo path (crate-pull-request)
- Document {{ enabled_tools_args }} template marker in AGENTS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip unrecognized safe-output tool names in enabled-tools args

Previously, unrecognized tool names (e.g. typos like "crate-pull-request")
were warned about but still appended to --enabled-tools. This caused the
real tool (create-pull-request) to be silently filtered out at runtime
because it was absent from the enabled list.

Now unrecognized names are skipped entirely, making the warning and
behavior consistent. The warning message is also updated to be clearer.

Additional improvements:
- Add test asserting ALL_KNOWN_SAFE_OUTPUTS covers every router-registered
  tool, preventing the list from drifting when new tools are added
- Add integration test verifying --enabled-tools flags appear in compiled
  pipeline YAML (end-to-end: standalone.rs + generate_enabled_tools_args +
  template substitution)
- Document is_safe_tool_name newline-safety requirement
- Add explanatory comment in base.yml template for the inline substitution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip non-MCP keys and warn on all-unrecognized safe-outputs

Address PR review feedback:

- Skip non-MCP safe-output keys (e.g. `memory`) from --enabled-tools
  generation. These keys have no MCP route, so including them would
  cause real MCP tools to be filtered out at runtime.

- Add prominent warning when all user-specified safe-output keys are
  invalid/unrecognized/non-MCP, since the agent would be restricted
  to diagnostic tools only.

- Remove unreachable `args.is_empty()` branch — ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS
  guarantees the args string is never empty when safe-outputs is
  configured.

- Add tests for memory key skipping and memory-only configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: memory-only safe-outputs should not restrict MCP tools

When safe-outputs contains only non-MCP keys (e.g. just `memory`) or
only unrecognized names (typos), return empty args so all tools remain
available — matching backward-compatible behavior. Previously this
path would emit only ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS in --enabled-tools, silently
restricting the agent to 4 diagnostic tools.

Also:
- Add debug! log in server-side filtering for enabled-tools entries
  that have no matching route, aiding troubleshooting
- Add template comment documenting positional arg ordering requirement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: derive safe-output tool lists from types at compile time

Replace hand-maintained string arrays with compile-time type-derived
lists using two new macros:

- tool_names![Type1, Type2, ...] → extracts ToolResult::NAME from
  each type into a &[&str] array
- all_safe_output_names![types...; "extra"] → combines type names
  with non-MCP string keys

Changes:
- Add ToolResult::REQUIRES_WRITE associated constant (default false)
- Extend tool_result! macro with `write = true` parameter (4 arms)
- Annotate all 17 write-requiring tools with write = true
- CreatePrResult (manual impl) also gets REQUIRES_WRITE = true
- Move WRITE_REQUIRING_SAFE_OUTPUTS from common.rs to tools/mod.rs
- Derive ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS, WRITE_REQUIRING_SAFE_OUTPUTS, and
  ALL_KNOWN_SAFE_OUTPUTS from type lists — no more string duplication
- Add NON_MCP_SAFE_OUTPUT_KEYS as public const in mod.rs
- Add 5 subset/consistency tests:
  - WRITE_REQUIRING ⊆ ALL_KNOWN
  - ALWAYS_ON ⊆ ALL_KNOWN
  - NON_MCP ⊆ ALL_KNOWN
  - REQUIRES_WRITE consistency (true for write tools, false for diag)
  - ALL_KNOWN count = write + diagnostic + non-MCP

Adding a new tool now requires adding its type to the list in mod.rs;
the name string is derived automatically from ToolResult::NAME,
eliminating the risk of typo drift between lists.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: document 1ES safe-outputs filtering gap and clarify HashSet purpose

- Add note in onees.rs explaining that 1ES target does not support
  --enabled-tools filtering (uses service connections, not mcp-http)
- Clarify HashSet comment in generate_enabled_tools_args — it deduplicates
  across user keys and ALWAYS_ON_TOOLS, not within HashMap keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct all_safe_output_names! doc comment and rename variable

- Fix doc comment: example showed two semicolons but macro only accepts
  one. Clarify that all types go before the semicolon, string keys after.
- Rename user_tool_count → effective_mcp_tool_count for clarity — it
  counts recognized, valid, non-MCP keys, not all user-specified keys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: promote unmatched enabled-tools log to warn and add disjointness checks

- Promote debug! to warn! for --enabled-tools entries with no matching
  route, matching the compile-time warning severity. A typo like
  "crate-pull-request" would otherwise be invisible in production logs.
- Add explicit disjointness assertions in test_all_known_completeness
  so overlapping lists produce a clear "tool X in both lists" message
  rather than a confusing count mismatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use MSAL token for chunked upload auth instead of localStorage

The chunked upload service was reading auth tokens from
localStorage.getItem('auth_token'), a key that is never set by the
MSAL-based auth flow. This caused all chunked upload requests to send
no Authorization header, resulting in 401 Unauthorized responses.

Fix: export the shared getAuthHeaders() from api.ts (which uses MSAL
acquireTokenSilent()) and import it in chunked-upload.ts, replacing
the broken localStorage-based implementation. All three call sites
updated to await the now-async function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix BearSSL build on Linux: header conflicts and linker shims

- l_tls.h: undef/restore l_os.h macro overrides around bearssl includes
  to prevent conflicts with system <string.h> (strerror, memcpy, etc.)
- l_tls.h: add libc shims for BearSSL internals (getentropy, open, read,
  close, time, __errno_location) mapping to l_os.h equivalents
- Taskfile: compile BearSSL with -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=0 to prevent
  __memcpy_chk/__memset_chk references in freestanding builds

Fixes fresh-clone build failures on Linux gcc and clang.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix parse_version to handle semver pre-release tags

parse_version('0.1.0-alpha.1') was returning (0,0,0) because
int('0-alpha') fails. Now strips pre-release suffix and leading 'v'
before parsing: '0.1.0-alpha.1' → (0, 1, 0).

This was preventing the auto-updater from correctly comparing versions
when VERSION contains pre-release identifiers.

ci(tooling): Generate release notes via Copilot CLI

The release workflow was calling the GitHub Models REST API directly to
generate polished release notes. This approach was brittle: it required
a dedicated PAT with Copilot entitlements, the API returned 403 on
accounts without the right plan, and there was no structured fallback
beyond a minimal bullet list — meaning any failure silently degraded
the release page quality.

The new approach has three stages:

1. A deterministic Node script (scripts/generate-release-notes.mjs)
   always runs first. It parses the git log and the PR metadata already
   collected by the workflow's github-script step, and produces a fully
   structured Markdown draft following the exact changelog format
   (prefix symbols, sections, contributors) but with a placeholder
   comment where the LLM summary paragraph should go. This draft is
   both the input to stage 2 and the high-quality fallback for stage 3.

2. The Copilot CLI (copilot -p ... --no-ask-user) is installed on the
   runner and asked to replace the placeholder with a polished 2-4
   sentence summary and write the result to release-notes.md. It reads
   the SKILL instructions, the commit context, and the deterministic
   draft from the filesystem — no external API call or secret beyond
   the COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN PAT is needed.

3. If the CLI exits non-zero or does not write the output file, the
   deterministic draft is used as-is. This guarantees that every release
   always gets correctly formatted notes regardless of LLM availability.

The release-notes prompt has been migrated from the ad-hoc workflow
asset (.github/workflows/assets/release-notes-prompt.md) to a SKILL
(.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md) so it can be discovered and
reused by the Copilot CLI and any future automation.

A vitest test suite (scripts/generate-release-notes.spec.mjs, 41 tests)
covers all deterministic generation logic: commit parsing, type-to-prefix
mapping, conventional subject parsing, backtick escaping, PR link
rendering, contributor deduplication, and edge cases (no commits,
non-conventional subjects).

commit.message:
Remove silent source file exclusion during Roslyn compilation (#80)

* Remove silent source file exclusion during Roslyn compilation

Previously, when Roslyn compilation failed, the runner would silently
retry by dropping files with direct errors and recompiling the remainder.
This could produce a passing run that was missing whole codeunits — a
silent false positive that is hard to detect and debug.

Now compilation is all-or-nothing: any error causes an immediate hard
failure with all Roslyn errors printed to stderr. Users get a clear
signal that compilation failed and exactly which files/lines are broken.

Changes:
- Remove RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles dictionary
- Remove RoslynCompiler.GetExclusionInfo() method
- Remove iterative 5-round exclusion retry loop in CompileFromTrees
- Compilation errors now always print to stderr (not verbose-only)
- Remove GetExclusionInfo() call sites in MockCodeunitHandle and
  MockRecordHandle (replaced with plain 'not found' messages)
- Add two unit tests in PipelineTests:
  RoslynCompiler_MultipleFiles_ReturnsNullWhenAnyFileHasErrors
  RoslynCompiler_MultipleFiles_ReturnsNullWhenSecondFileHasErrors

Closes #66

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: clean up ExcludedFiles remnants and test quality

- Remove all remaining RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles usages in Program.cs
  (PrintCompilationGaps call and TryReportPipelineGapsAsync argument)
- Remove Executor.PrintCompilationGaps() method (now dead code)
- Update TelemetryReporter.TryReportPipelineGapsAsync to drop the
  compilationGaps parameter and all compilation-gap telemetry logic
- Make RoslynCompiler.Compile(List<(Name,SyntaxTree)>) internal so the
  test project no longer needs explicit Microsoft.CodeAnalysis References
- Remove fragile bin-path Microsoft.CodeAnalysis Reference items from
  AlRunner.Tests.csproj
- Print all Roslyn errors on compilation failure (remove Take(30) cap)
- Rename 'RED tests' comment to 'Regression tests for Issue #66'
- Remove .cs extension from test source Name values (Compile appends .cs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update test-matrix.yml to run each test case individually using a for loop,

commit.message:
fix: distinguish compilation errors from test failures in --output-json (#67) (#81)

* fix: distinguish compilation errors from test failures in --output-json

Fixes #67.

When a codeunit or record type is excluded from Roslyn compilation (e.g.
because it depends on an XmlPort or other unsupported BC object type), the
runner previously reported the resulting runtime exception as status: "fail"
in the JSON output. This was misleading: "fail" should mean an assertion
failure in test logic, not a tooling/configuration problem.

Changes:
- Add CompilationExcludedException (AlRunner.Runtime namespace), thrown by
  MockCodeunitHandle and MockRecordHandle when a compiled-out type is invoked.
- Executor.RunTests catches CompilationExcludedException alongside
  NotSupportedException and maps both to TestStatus.Error instead of Fail.
- SerializeJsonOutput: status "error" is emitted for Error results; the
  top-level "errors" counter is populated; "failed" now counts only real
  assertion failures.
- New top-level "compilationErrors" array in JSON output lists each excluded
  C# file and its Roslyn diagnostics (omitted when empty/null).
- Pipeline.RunCore passes RoslynCompiler.ExcludedFiles to SerializeJsonOutput.
- Server.cs SerializeServerResponse includes compilationErrors field.
- --help and --guide updated with status value documentation.
- Test case tests/84-json-compilation-error: XmlPort alongside a passing
  codeunit; verifies compilationErrors is populated in JSON output.
- AlRunner.Tests/JsonOutputTests.cs: 9 unit/integration tests.

commit.message:
Fix AlwaysOnTop sound playing when pin/unpin fails (#46910)

## Summary of the Pull Request

`ProcessCommand` played the pin/unpin sound unconditionally, regardless
of whether `SetWindowPos` succeeded. This caused spurious audio feedback
when targeting desktop, taskbar, task view, start menu, or elevated
windows from a non-elevated process.

Gate sound playback on actual state change:

```cpp
bool stateChanged = false;
// ...
if (UnpinTopmostWindow(window)) { stateChanged = true; /* ... */ }
// ...
if (PinTopmostWindow(window))   { stateChanged = true; /* ... */ }
// ...
if (stateChanged && AlwaysOnTopSettings::settings()->enableSound)
    m_sound.Play(soundType);
```

## PR Checklist

- [ ] **Communication:** I've discussed this with core contributors
already. If the work hasn't been agreed, this work might be rejected
- [ ] **Tests:** Added/updated and all pass
- [ ] **Localization:** All end-user-facing strings can be localized
- [ ] **Dev docs:** Added/updated
- [ ] **New binaries:** Added on the required places
- [ ] [JSON for
signing](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/ESRPSigning_core.json)
for new binaries
- [ ] [WXS for
installer](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/installer/PowerToysSetup/Product.wxs)
for new binaries and localization folder
- [ ] [YML for CI
pipeline](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/ci/templates/build-powertoys-steps.yml)
for new test projects
- [ ] [YML for signed
pipeline](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/release.yml)
- [ ] **Documentation updated:** If checked, please file a pull request
on [our docs
repo](https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/windows-uwp/tree/docs/hub/powertoys)
and link it here: #xxx

## Detailed Description of the Pull Request / Additional comments

`PinTopmostWindow` and `UnpinTopmostWindow` already return `bool`
indicating success, and the existing code already branches on those
return values for bookkeeping and telemetry — but the sound playback at
the end of `ProcessCommand` ignored the result. Added a `stateChanged`
flag set only inside the success branches, then checked before calling
`m_sound.Play()`.

## Validation Steps Performed

- Verified that the `soundType` / `stateChanged` logic covers all four
paths: pin success, pin failure, unpin success, unpin failure.
- Code review passed with no comments.

fix: 9 critical/high backend bugs in Hospital Network module

Fix 1: Wrap createNetwork() two INSERTs in a pg transaction
- Uses database.getPool().connect() + BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
- Prevents orphaned networks when member INSERT fails

Fix 2: Add UNIQUE index on hospital_networks.id_prefix in database.ts
- CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_hospital_networks_id_prefix
- Runs in seedDefaultSettings() on every startup (idempotent)

Fix 3: Validate id_prefix uniqueness BEFORE the transaction
- UPPER() case-insensitive check before any writes
- Throws descriptive error rather than letting DB constraint bubble up

Fix 4: Add authorization checks to enrollAnimal()
- Verifies hospitalId (if provided) is an active member of the network
- Verifies enrolledBy user is a network member OR hospital staff
- Uses UNION query against hospital_network_members + hospital_staff

Fix 5: Correct network_type enum in frontend
- 'academic' -> 'cooperative' to match DB CHECK constraint
- DB only allows: private, government, ngo, cooperative, franchise

Fix 6: Add acceptWalkInInvite() with expiry and status validation
- New service method checks expires_at and status === 'pending'

Fix 7: Add network deactivation endpoint
- PATCH /hospital-networks/:id/deactivate (authMiddleware)
- Service checks corporate_admin role or platform admin
- Controller and route added alongside existing network routes

Fix 8: COALESCE null animal names in audit log SELECT
- COALESCE(a.name, '[Animal ID: ' || cal.animal_id || ']')
- Prevents NULL showing in audit log when animal is deleted

Fix 9: Add pagination to listNetworkHospitals()
- Signature: listNetworkHospitals(networkId, page=1, limit=20)

- Controller reads page/limit from query params

commit.message:
fix: artwork blank if url contains space (#781)

Spotify returns thumbnail URLs with literal spaces (e.g.
`https://seed-mix-image.spotifycdn.com/v6/img/desc/Background Dinner
Party Instrumental/en/default`), which are invalid in CSS `url()` and
cause `Invalid property value` errors — rendering blank artwork squares.

## Changes

- **`player-artwork-utils.ts`** — Apply `encodeURI()` to entity image
and fallback image URLs in `getBackgroundImage` and
`getBackgroundImageUrl`
- **`media-browse-utils.ts`** — Same encoding in `renderFavoritesItem`
template
- **`favorites-icons.ts`** — Same encoding in `imageStyle` construction
in `render()`

```ts
// Before
return `background-image: url(${image.entityImage})`;

// After
return `background-image: url(${encodeURI(image.entityImage)})`;
```

fix(firmware): update installed_version immediately after install completes

After detecting the new version in the poll loop, immediately write
coordinator._health_data["version"] and notify health listeners so the
HA entity's installed_version reflects the change without waiting for
the next health poll cycle (~5 s).

Also call async_update() on success so _latest_version is refreshed
from the Pi's update endpoint, flipping the update card to "up to date"
right away instead of waiting for the hourly poll.

Add test: test_async_install_updates_coordinator_health_immediately
  - verifies coordinator._health_data["version"] == new version
  - verifies _notify_health_listeners() was called
